# Support

## Getting Help

The AegisAI Enterprise DevSecOps Control Center is an internal platform tool. Support channels are listed below.

### Documentation

Start here before asking for help:
- [Project Context](../PROJECT_CONTEXT.md) — governing document (Single Source of Truth)
- [System Architecture](../SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md) — technical blueprint
- [Engineering Standards](../ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md) — coding and design standards
- [Dashboard Documentation](../docs/releases/README.md) — release notes index, changelog, architecture decisions, known limitations

### Community

- **GitHub Issues:** Use [bug reports](https://github.com/aegisai/dashboard/issues/new?labels=bug&template=bug_report.yml) and [feature requests](https://github.com/aegisai/dashboard/issues/new?labels=enhancement&template=feature_request.yml)
- **Internal Slack:** #aegisai-dev channel
- **Office Hours:** Weekly platform engineering office hours (see calendar)

### Security

Report security vulnerabilities privately to **security@aegisai.internal** — see [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md).

## Issue Resolution SLA

| Priority | Response Time | Resolution Target |
|---|---|---|
| P0 (Critical) | 2 hours | 24 hours |
| P1 (High) | 4 hours | 3 days |
| P2 (Medium) | 1 business day | 2 weeks |
| P3 (Low) | 3 business days | Next sprint |

## Before Opening an Issue

1. Check [existing issues](https://github.com/aegisai/dashboard/issues) — yours may already be reported.
2. Check [known limitations](../docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md) — the issue may be accepted.
3. Check [the roadmap](../docs/ROADMAP.md) — the feature may already be planned.
4. Gather relevant logs, version info, and reproduction steps.

## Feature Requests

Feature requests should follow the [feature request template](https://github.com/aegisai/dashboard/issues/new?labels=enhancement&template=feature_request.yml).
All proposals are reviewed during sprint planning.
